mapping.a_element: Cliffordization: the twisted/deformed Hopf-algebra product that reorganizes operator products
mapping.b_element: twisted products (Rota cliffordizations) induced by branching operators in the symmetric-function algebra

mapping.a_element: Wick's theorem / Wick normal re-ordering of quantum fields
mapping.b_element: re-expression of symmetric-function products under the twisted (cliffordized) product, e.g. yielding orthogonal/symplectic-type bases

mapping.a_element: underlying Hopf algebra of QFT field operators
mapping.b_element: Hopf and bi-algebra structure of the ring of symmetric functions

mapping.a_element: Rota-Stein combinatorial formula driving the reordering
mapping.b_element: branching operators / Laplace pairing generating the twisted product

mapping.a_element: closed algebraic expressions obtained from the Hopf-algebraic identity
mapping.b_element: explicit symmetric-function operations realizing the same cliffordization

brief_justification: Both papers are built on the identical machinery of 'Cliffordization' — deforming a Hopf algebra's product into a twisted product via a Laplace-pairing/branching cocycle (the Rota-Stein construction); paper A uses it to realize Wick's theorem for QFT operators, and paper B uses the very same Rota cliffordization, induced by branching operators, on the Hopf algebra of symmetric functions, so the governing algebraic structure corresponds role-for-role.
